questions.question: 中文导读：What advantage does SVD have over eigendecomposition?
options: 中文选项：SVD is always faster to compute
中文选项：SVD works on any matrix of any shape, while eigendecomposition requires square matrices with full eigenvector sets
中文选项：SVD produces smaller output matrices
中文选项：SVD does not require numerical computation
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Eigendecomposition only works on square matrices that have n linearly independent eigenvectors. SVD decomposes ANY m x n matrix into U * Sigma * V^T with no restrictions on shape or rank.

questions.question: 中文导读：What does 'low-rank approximation' mean?
options: 中文选项：Removing rows with low values from a matrix
中文选项：Approximating a matrix by keeping only its most important components, producing a simpler matrix with fewer independent directions
中文选项：Converting a matrix to a lower precision data type
中文选项：Sorting the rows of a matrix by their magnitude
questions.explanation: 中文解析：A rank-k approximation keeps only the top k singular values and their vectors, discarding the rest. The Eckart-Young theorem proves this is the BEST possible approximation of that rank.

questions.question: 中文导读：In SVD A = U * Sigma * V^T, what geometric operation does each factor represent?
options: 中文选项：U scales, Sigma rotates, V^T translates
中文选项：V^T rotates in input space, Sigma scales along principal axes, U rotates into output space
中文选项：U compresses, Sigma expands, V^T normalizes
中文选项：All three factors perform the same operation: rotation
questions.explanation: 中文解析：SVD reveals that every matrix performs: (1) V^T rotates inputs to align with principal directions, (2) Sigma stretches/compresses along each axis, (3) U rotates the result into the output space. Rotate, scale, rotate.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why does sklearn implement PCA using SVD instead of eigendecomposition of the covariance matrix?
options: 中文选项：SVD produces different results that are more accurate for ML
中文选项：SVD works directly on the data matrix without forming the covariance matrix, avoiding squaring the condition number and improving numerical stability
中文选项：SVD is easier to parallelize on GPUs
中文选项：SVD does not require centering the data
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Forming A^T*A squares the singular values (and condition number). If A has singular values [1000, 0.001], A^T*A has eigenvalues [10^6, 10^-6] -- 6 digits of precision lost. SVD avoids this by working directly on A.

questions.question: 中文导读：How does truncated SVD enable recommendation systems to predict missing ratings?
options: 中文选项：It fills missing entries with the average rating
中文选项：It decomposes the ratings matrix into latent user and movie profiles; the dot product of a user profile with a movie profile predicts the missing rating
中文选项：It clusters similar users together and copies their ratings
中文选项：It trains a neural network on the observed ratings
questions.explanation: 中文解析：SVD decomposes the ratings matrix into user profiles (U), latent factor importance (Sigma), and movie profiles (V^T). The low-rank reconstruction fills in missing entries based on the latent factors (genre, era, style) that explain user preferences.
